title: skopeo-copy: docker-archive: multitag support
body:
Add multitag support when generating docker-archive tarballs via the
newly added '--aditional-tag' option, which can be specified multiple
times to add more than one tag.  All specified tags will be added to the
RepoTags field in the docker-archive's manifest.json file.

This change requires to vendor the latest containers/image with
commit a1a9391830fd08637edbe45133fd0a8a2682ae75.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <vrothberg@suse.com>

comments.body:
```
---> Making bundle: validate-vet
# command-line-arguments
cmd/skopeo/copy.go:22:20: undefined: contextFromGlobalOptions
cmd/skopeo/copy.go:27:25: undefined: contextFromGlobalOptions
cmd/skopeo/copy.go:40:24: undefined: getPolicyContext
make: *** [validate] Error 2
```

Having looked at other PRs, I am under the impression that something's wrong with the CI atm. Could it be related to the latest Fedora 28 release?

> Having looked at other PRs, I am under the impression that something's wrong with the CI atm.

I agree, looking into it now.

> Could it be related to the latest Fedora 28 release?

I’ve already fixed the immediate breakage caused by updating to F28. This is something a bit different, I can’t reproduce it locally.

Actually, you’re right, this was directly caused by the F28 update.  Should be fixed by https://github.com/projectatomic/skopeo/pull/503 (per tests in https://github.com/projectatomic/skopeo/pull/502 ).

Please rebase this PR on top of `master`, then the tests should pass.
@mtrmac Awesome, thanks for fixing it! I just rebased the change.

@mtrmac @runcom Could we prepare a new release of skopeo including this change? We need that for the Open Build Service, and I'd prefer to bump our package to a new version rather than backporting the change.
